title: Commutative Property of Multiplication
essential_question: How can you use the Commutative Property of Multiplication to find products?
strategies: Changing the order of factors does not change the product
Use arrays turned sideways to prove

items.question: Which property says you can multiply in any order?
3 × 8 = 24. What other fact does this tell you?
An array has 2 rows of 9. Turn it. Now it has 9 rows of 2. Are the products the same?
choices: B. No, first is 18, second is 11

items.title: The Commutative Property
items.content: When you multiply two numbers, you can change their order and the product stays the same. 3 × 5 = 15 and 5 × 3 = 15. This is called the Commutative Property of Multiplication.

items.title: Why It Works — Arrays
items.content: A 3×5 array has 3 rows of 5. Turn it sideways and you get a 5×3 array (5 rows of 3). Same total objects! That's why the product doesn't change.

items.title: Using the Property to Remember Facts
items.content: If you know 7 × 4 = 28, you automatically know 4 × 7 = 28 too! This cuts the number of facts you need to memorize almost in half.

items.question: Jake says 3 × 7 and 7 × 3 have different products. Is he correct?
Which pair of facts shows the Commutative Property?
items.explanation: Same factors, switched order, same product.

items.question: 4 × 9 = ?. Use the Commutative Property. If you know 9 × 4 = 36, then 4 × 9 = ?
An array has 6 rows and 4 columns. Turn it. What are the new dimensions?
Lisa knows these facts: 2×8=16, 3×8=24, 5×8=40. Which fact does she also know for free?
Does the Commutative Property work for subtraction? Is 8−3 = 3−8?
items.explanation: Commutative Property only works for addition and multiplication, not subtraction.

items.question: Tom says there are 100 multiplication facts from 1×1 to 10×10. Using the Commutative Property, how many unique facts do you actually need to memorize? (hint: half + the 'square' facts)
items.explanation: There are 10 square facts (1×1, 2×2...10×10) and 90 non-square facts. The 90 non-square facts pair up into 45 pairs. 45+10=55 unique facts.

items.question: If a × b = 42 and a = 6, what is b? And what is b × a?
